title: DIY Bucket or Basket Shelf Display Stand
summary: A simple angled A-frame display stand built from two identical 1x3 frames, sized to hold a row of buckets or baskets at a lean.
description:
The whole stand is just two identical 45°-angled frames joined together, which is what makes this one of the fastest builds in the catalog — get the 45° angle right once (a scrap block cut to that angle, or a speed square, both work as a guide) and every joint in both frames repeats the same cut. The two frames lean the buckets back at a display angle rather than standing them upright, which is the entire trick behind why loose buckets or baskets sit securely on it without any hardware holding them in place — gravity and the lean do the work.

The cross support, scribed to fit after the frames are joined rather than cut to a fixed measurement up front, is what keeps the stand rigid side to side. Scribing it in place (tracing the actual angle off the assembled frame) is more reliable than trusting a calculated angle, since small variations in how square each frame came out will change that angle slightly.

materials.name: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or 2" self-tapping screws
Buckets or baskets
materials.note: Not included in the cost estimate — pick sizes that fit your own frame spacing.

cutList.note: Scribed to fit after the frames are assembled, not cut to a fixed length up front.

steps.title: Build the two side frames
steps.body: Build two identical A-frames from the frame boards and cross braces. Use a scrap block cut at 45°, or a speed square, to guide placing the boards at the correct angle. Attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws or 2" self-tapping screws, gluing every joint.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or 2" self-tapping screws

steps.title: Join the frames and add the cross support
steps.body: Attach the two frames together with 1-1/4" nails, staples, or screws and glue. Lay a scrap board over the sides, trace the actual angle where it crosses, cut to that scribed line, and attach for additional side-to-side rigidity.

steps.title: Load the stand
steps.body: Set your buckets or baskets into the angled frames — the lean is what holds them in place with no extra hardware.

images.alt: Angled A-frame stand displaying a row of buckets
